title: paraprasophyllum argillaceum
side_effects: Paraprasophyllum argillaceum can have side effects if used improperly, such as causing gastrointestinal issues, allergic reactions, and skin irritation. Ingesting the plant's sap may lead to nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, while skin contact can result in redness, itching, and inflammation. Overuse of the plant's extracts can also cause liver and kidney damage, as well as interact negatively with certain medications. If used in excess or without proper guidance, Paraprasophyllum argillaceum can have serious and potentially long-lasting consequences for one's health.
